channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rihanna attends the trial of her partner, rapper A$AP Rocky, who is accused in a 2021 shooting
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — In a stretch of questioning crucial to the defense of A$AP Rocky, his lawyer on Friday challenged the account of his accuser, who said he suffered a hand injury when the hip-hop performer fired a gun at him in 2021.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about the NTSB, the agency investigating the DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people brought renewed focus on the federal agency charged with investigating aviation disasters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: Texas ordered a ban on the Chinese AI startup DeepSeek on government devices days after the popular chatbot has shook investors and the tech community in recent weeks for its ability to compete with OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Carolina puts inmate Marion Bowman Jr. to death in state’s third execution since September
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina put a third inmate to death in four months Friday as the state goes through a backlog of prisoners who exhausted their appeals while the state couldn’t find lethal injection drugs, Marion Bowman Jr. was executed at 6:27 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former Fox Sports reporter Julie Stewart-Binks accuses a top executive of sexual assault
item.description: A former Fox Sports reporter and anchor filed a lawsuit Friday against the network and top executive Charlie Dixon, saying he sexually assaulted her after coaxing her up to his hotel room to discuss Super Bowl plans in 2016 and was later pushed out of her job for fighting back.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Latest snowpack survey reveals California could be in for extremely dry year
item.description: California’s snowpack has fallen well below average following an extremely dry January, according to the Department of Water Resources’ second snow survey of the season. The survey was conducted Friday at Phillips Station in the Sierra Nevada near Sacramento.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: It costs the most to rent an apartment in these US cities: report
item.description: The price of renting an apartment is on the rise, particularly in some major U.S. cities. This is according to the latest rent report from Zumper, which has found that across the nation, the price for a one-bedroom apartment is up 2.5% since this time last year.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he and Putin could do something 'significant' toward ending Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said his administration has already had “very serious” discussions with Russia about its war in Ukraine and that he and Russian President Vladimir Putin could soon take “significant” action toward ending the grinding conflict.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: ICE agents facing escalating threats of violence
item.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection is taking them seriously enough to circulate a memo among agents and officers within the agency.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Tour guide at former Colorado gold mine fell out of elevator after apparently not latching door
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A tour guide killed at a former Colorado gold mine last year fell out of a crowded elevator taking tourists below ground after apparently not latching its door closed, documents obtained by The Associated Press show.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Delphi murders: Convicted killer in hiking trail slayings cites new evidence in bid to overturn conviction
item.description: Delphi murders suspect Richard Allen, found guilty of brutally murdering two teen girls on an Indiana hiking trail in 2017, is asking a Carroll County court to overturn his conviction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man agrees to plead guilty for flying drone that damaged firefighting aircraft in LA wildfire
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The pilot of a drone that crashed into a firefighting plane, leaving a gaping hole and grounding the aircraft during the deadly Palisades Fire in Los Angeles, has agreed to plead guilty to a misdemeanor count of unsafely operating a drone, federal authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump vows sweeping tariffs on oil, pharmaceuticals and semiconductors
item.description: President Trump on Friday vowed to impose sweeping tariffs on semiconductor chips, pharmaceuticals, steel and aluminum and oil and gas.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Air traffic controller left early from work before crash: report
item.description: An air traffic controller at Reagan National Airport reportedly left early on Wednesday before the deadly collision between an American Airlines flight and a military helicopter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US colleges returning to campus sexual assault rules created during Trump's first term
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Schools and universities responding to complaints of sexual misconduct must return to policies created during President Donald Trump's first term, with requirements for live hearings and more protections for accused students, according to new guidance issued Friday by the Education
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Robert Santos decides to resign as US Census Bureau director midway through a 5-year term
item.description: Robert Santos, who emphasized inclusivity and outreach to overlooked communities, has decided to resign as director of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina's ex-lieutenant governor dismisses lawsuit against CNN, has no plans to run again
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina's former Lt. Gov. Mark Robinson on Friday withdrew his defamation lawsuit against CNN that challenged a report about him making explicit posts on a pornography website’s message board over a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Parents charged with neglect after 8-year-old girl dies from fentanyl poisoning
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS -- Two Indianapolis parents have been arrested and charged with neglect resulting in her death after their 8-year-old child died from acute fentanyl poisoning.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: DC plane crash: Military aircraft collisions raise questions about training and equipment, expert says
item.description: U.S. military helicopter crashes like the one that took down a commercial American Airlines flight on Wednesday night are rare but have increased since 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flight recorders recovered in DCA crash
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) said Thursday that they recovered flight data recorders, known as black boxes, from the aircraft involved in the deadly mid-air collision near Reagan Washington National Airport earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Black History Month explained: Its origins, celebrations and myths
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Beginning Feb. 1, schools, museums and communities across the nation will mark the start of Black History Month –- a celebration of Black history, culture and education.
item.dc:creator: HAYA PANJWANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump aid freeze disrupts global HIV/AIDS fight
item.description: A legacy bipartisan initiative to combat HIV and AIDS in Africa is collateral damage from President Donald Trump’s directive to halt all U.S. foreign assistance, despite efforts to exempt humanitarian assistance and lifesaving medication from being caught up in the three-month funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says helicopter in crash was flying too high
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday asserted the military helicopter involved in a deadly midair collision near Washington, D.C., was "flying too high.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Congress authorized more flights at Reagan National despite warnings
item.description: Congress added more flights to Reagan National's schedule last year despite warnings from the airport.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DC plane crash: What to know about the mistakes made leading up to the fatal collision
item.description: As the National Transportation Safety Board investigates the fatal midair collision that left 67 dead, details are emerging about mistakes and failures leading up to the crash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump warns Canada, Mexico tariffs are coming on Saturday
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday said he plans to follow through on Saturday on his threat to impose 25 percent tariffs on goods from Mexico and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: OnePlus 13 review: excellent, but not perfect
item.description: Over the years, OnePlus has made some memorable phones with unique features, like a pop-up selfie camera, McLaren editions, and super-fast wireless charging. This time around, they're making the OnePlus 13 the most water resistant phone I've ever tested.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: DC plane crash: Recovery continues, NTSB says investigation ongoing
item.description: Air crash investigations can take months, and federal investigators told reporters they would not speculate on the cause.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Civil rights attorney from Wichita, Kansas, victim of plane crash in DC
item.description: A woman who made a name for herself in Wichita and was in the process of doing the same thing in Washington was on the plane that crashed in Washington, D.C., on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Payton Steiner, NewsNation

item.title: Dick Button, Olympic great and voice of skating, dies at 95
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dick Button was more than the most accomplished men’s figure skater in history. He was one of his sport’s great innovators and promoters, the man responsible not only for inventing the flying camel spin but describing it to generations of fans.
item.dc:creator: BARRY WILNER and DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Collision between passenger jet and Army helicopter near DC leaves no survivors
item.description: A collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport near Washington, D.C, killed 67 people, including more than a dozen figure skaters.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Engineering student among DC plane crash victims
item.description: DAYTON, Ohio (WDTN) -- Cedarville University is grieving one of its own after the devastating plane crash in Washington D.C. Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Lewis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Honolulu police have arrested 6 people in connection with deadly New Year’s fireworks blast
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Police arrested a total of six people in connection with a deadly explosion of illegal fireworks in a Honolulu neighborhood on New Year’s Eve and confiscated 500 pounds (227 kilograms) of unused fireworks from the scene of the blast, authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans leave Florida frustrated with lack of final plan to pass Trump agenda
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) arrived at this week’s House Republican retreat with hopes of uniting the fractious GOP conference around a plan to pass President Trump's agenda — but instead, the group is departing south Florida with rising tensions in its ranks. Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans advance Trump OMB nominee as Democrats boycott vote
item.description: Senate Republicans advanced the nomination of Russell Vought to lead the White House budget office as Democrats boycotted the meeting to protest the administration's recent efforts to freeze large swaths of federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Aviation expert weighs in on how aircraft crash near DC may have happened
item.description: An aviation expert told Nexstar's WDCW that the altitude of both the passenger plane and Army helicopter may have played a principal role in the aircraft collision near Reagan National Airport (DCA).
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump casts blame during briefing on deadly plane crash
item.description: President Donald Trump started Thursday’s press briefing with a moment of silence for the victims and their families after a deadly crash involving an American Airlines plane and an Army helicopter near Ronald Reagan National Airport on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, Fox 5
